title: limonium binervosum subsp. lychnidifolium
intro: Limonium binervosum subsp. lychnidifolium, also known as the sea lavender or sea pink, is a plant species within the Plumbaginaceae family, characterized by its fragrant, pink or purple flowers and narrow, linear leaves. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antihistamine effects. The health benefits of sea lavender include relief from respiratory issues, such as asthma and allergies, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and promote relaxation. Herbal preparations of sea lavender can be found in tea, oil, and tincture forms, which can be used to support overall well-being and alleviate various health concerns.
